reasoning: Paid loss triangle — expect materially longer tail than incurred loss. Selected starting age 203 as least problematic of available options: is_monotone_from_here=False prevents ideal selection, but age 203 shows fewer slope_sign_changes (3 vs 6 at age 143) and lower cv_at_starting_age (0.0108 vs 0.0182). Bondy method selected (simple last observed factor approach) because tail is immaterial at 0.0616% of CDF, qualifying for materiality anchor. No curve fitting required when materiality anchor applies and gap_flag=False. Last observed factor at age 203 stable across early AYs (range 1.0021–1.0327). Alternative scenarios evaluated: double_exp (R²=0.4658, marginal, inf in LOO) rejected due to weak diagnostics; skurnick (tail=1.3291, R²=0.7953) rejected as implausible extreme; modified_bondy variants rejected for exceeding materiality threshold (>0.1% of CDF); mcclenahan essentially unity, less informative than bondy. Monotonicity failure at both starting ages reflects late-age volatility inherent in paid loss development, common at extended durations where incremental claims drive occasional upticks.
prior_delta_driver: Prior year selection not provided in context; comparison deferred to next analysis cycle.
alternatives_considered: Age 143 rejected: is_monotone_from_here=False, slope_sign_changes=6 (excessive structural breaks); exp_dev_quick (R²=0.231, poor fit, gap_flag=True, tail 0.476% material); exp_dev_exact_last (gap_flag=True); exp_dev_product (gap_flag=True); modified_bondy_double_dev (tail 0.123% exceeds materiality); modified_bondy_square_ratio (tail 0.123% exceeds materiality); double_exp at age 203 (R²=0.4658 below 0.75 threshold, inf in LOO_max); skurnick (tail 1.329 implausible, not casualty-appropriate); mcclenahan (unity output, no predictive value). Gap rule satisfied for bondy selection (gap_flag=False).
diagnostics_summary: Materiality anchor applies: tail 0.0616% << 0.1% of CDF threshold. No curve diagnostics needed for Bondy method. Observed last factor at age 203: stable pattern across accident years (range 1.0021–1.0327), mean ~1.01. Sensitivity analysis: +10% tail movement = $518k reserve delta (~0.07% of base), -10% = -$633k; ±20% movements produce -1.4M to +950k (materiality <1.5% at extreme). Gap to last observed = 0 (Bondy uses last factor directly). Closure data not provided; materiality anchor sufficient for age 203 selection.
